act.fb.2: v0.9.2 — tabular catalog grows from 7 to 15 classifiers.
act.lead: The latest releases from the repository auto-update from github.com/DashAISoftware/dashAI. For the monthly editorial digest, subscribe to the newsletter.
act.nl.fine: Public mailing list · unsubscribe anytime · 0 commercial emails.
act.nl.p: Once a month. New features, models added to the catalog, community plugins, relevant papers. Zero promos.
fcta.lead: dashAI is pre first-public-release. Every new component, every reported bug, every plugin published on PyPI counts. If you got this far, you're probably the right person to write the next one.
feat.lead: A sample of the 60 available models. All share the same twelve abstractions — the UI emerges from the schema.
hero.c1.p: Twelve base classes for registering models, loaders, metrics, optimizers and explainers. The interface is generated from the Pydantic schema; plugins are distributed via PyPI.
hero.c2.h: Local processing on the user's machine
hero.c2.p: Desktop application operating on user data. Does not require cloud infrastructure, external authentication or online dependencies for its core functions.
hero.c3.p: The project code is open-source, without copyleft clauses or reserved proprietary components. It is auditable, modifiable and derivable.
hero.c4.h: Integrated pipeline for analysis and modeling
hero.c4.p: A single visual interface covers import, data exploration, training, evaluation and explainability of predictive and generative models.
hero.h: Train Machine Learning models without writing code, on your own machine.
hero.sub: dashAI is a desktop application for data analysis and machine learning model training. It operates on local data and does not require cloud services, external authentication or API keys. It supports importing data, training predictive and generative models, evaluating them and applying explainability techniques in an environment under the user's control.
ins.anid.n: National Agency for Research and Development
inst.lead: dashAI is a Chilean academic project maintained by a network of research centers and public funding. There isn't a single company behind it. That's the point.
mod.g.d: Llama, Mistral, Qwen, PixArt-Sigma, SDXL. Open weights.
mod.m.d: Random Forest to transformers. Each one a subclass of BaseModel with its Pydantic schema.
mod.p.d: One click installs an extension from PyPI. No restart, no external shell. All under the same license.
mods.lead: Three core modules of the software. Each with its own color accent. Each explorable from this page.
sho.e1.p: Each form field is born from a Pydantic line. Type, range, default and description render automatically.
sho.e2.p: The new component touches zero React files. The UI shows up on its own once the backend registers it.
sho.e3.p: Tabular, NLP, translation, LLMs and generative vision coexist in one consistent UI — on the same twelve abstractions.
sho.h: This is what the interface looks like.
sho.lead: When you register a component, the UI isn't programmed: it's generated. The backend exposes its Pydantic schema and the frontend renders the form with no prior knowledge. This is what you see when you open dashAI.
sho.mock.ds.f4: 1247 values · 12 unique categories · no rare values
sho.mock.ds.status: Schema inferred from file · validation complete
sho.mock.f1: Number of trees in the forest.
sho.mock.f4: Use bootstrap samples when building each tree.
sho.mock.gen.f1: Sampling randomness control. Lower values = more deterministic outputs.
sho.mock.gen.f2: Maximum length of generated text (in tokens).
sho.mock.gen.f3: Nucleus sampling: discards tokens whose cumulative probability exceeds 1−top_p.
sho.mock.gen.f4: Return tokens as they are generated, rather than waiting for the full batch.
